definitions.definition: An electric current that periodically reverses its direction of flow, in contrast to direct current (DC) which flows in one direction.
definitions.example: Household electricity is an example of AC.

definitions.definition: An electric current that flows in one direction only, from positive to negative, without changing direction.
A voltage that periodically changes its magnitude and direction, similar to AC.
definitions.example: Alternating voltage is used in household electrical systems.

definitions.definition: A voltage that remains constant in magnitude and direction, similar to DC.
A method used to calculate the maximum value of AC using the cosine function.
definitions.example: The cosine method is used to find the maximum value of AC in a circuit.

definitions.definition: The square root of the average value of the square of a quantity, often used to express the effective value of AC.
definitions.example: The RMS value of AC is used to express its effective value in a circuit.

definitions.definition: The difference in time between the maximum or minimum points of two or more AC quantities.
definitions.example: Phase difference is used to describe the relationship between two AC quantities.

definitions.definition: The angle by which one AC quantity leads or lags another in a circuit.
definitions.example: Phase angle is used to describe the relationship between two AC quantities.

definitions.definition: When an AC quantity falls behind or lags another in a circuit.
definitions.example: Lagging phase occurs when an AC quantity is behind the other in a circuit.

definitions.definition: When an AC quantity leads or precedes another in a circuit.
definitions.example: Leading phase occurs when an AC quantity is ahead of the other in a circuit.

definitions.definition: The ratio of real power to apparent power in an AC circuit.
definitions.example: Power factor is used to describe the efficiency of an AC circuit.

definitions.definition: The product of root mean square (RMS) value of current and RMS value of voltage in an AC circuit.
definitions.example: Apparent power is used to describe the total power in an AC circuit.

definitions.definition: The actual power consumed by an AC circuit, measured in watts.
definitions.example: Real power is used to describe the actual power consumed by an AC circuit.

definitions.definition: A graphical representation of the relationship between apparent power, real power, and power factor in an AC circuit.
definitions.example: Power triangle is used to describe the relationship between apparent power, real power, and power factor in an AC circuit.

definitions.definition: A device that transfers electrical energy from one circuit to another through electromagnetic induction.
definitions.example: Transformers are used to increase or decrease voltage levels in an AC circuit.

definitions.definition: The opposition to current flow in an AC circuit due to the presence of an inductor.
definitions.example: Inductive reactance is used to describe the opposition to current flow in an AC circuit.

definitions.definition: The opposition to current flow in an AC circuit due to the presence of a capacitor.
definitions.example: Capacitive reactance is used to describe the opposition to current flow in an AC circuit.
